title: cryptogramma acrostichoides
intro: Cryptogramma acrostichoides, also known as the golden rockfoil or rockfoil, is a species of flowering plant in the family Saxifragaceae, commonly found in the western United States and Canada. This perennial plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it has been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cryptogramma acrostichoides are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce inflammation, and prevent the spread of infection. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, or salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
